notes: Baseline-B: FP32 all-DRAM tiled model using the same RTL 8x8 tile size; no BRAM reuse. LayerNorm true MACs are 0; detailed modeled operations are reported separately.

Baseline-B: FP32 all-DRAM tiled model using the same RTL 8x8 tile size; no BRAM reuse.

Baseline-B: FP32 all-DRAM tiled model using the same RTL 8x8 tile size; no BRAM reuse. Standard FP32 softmax all-DRAM memory model. True MACs are 0 because Softmax is non-GEMM; the operations column reports modeled row-wise softmax cost with FP32 exp/div initiation intervals exp_ii=4, div_ii=4. Baseline FP32 Softmax uses row-wise FSM latency with explicit exp/div IIs: rows*((2+exp_ii+div_ii)*Tp+overhead) = 1200*((2+4+4)*200+1).
Baseline-B: FP32 all-DRAM tiled model using the same RTL 8x8 tile size; no BRAM reuse.
Baseline-B: FP32 all-DRAM tiled model using the same RTL 8x8 tile size; no BRAM reuse.
Baseline-B: FP32 all-DRAM tiled model using the same RTL 8x8 tile size; no BRAM reuse. No FC1+GELU fusion: hidden activation is materialized in DRAM.

Optimized INT8 RTL-aware model: 8x8 shared systolic, physical buffer lifetime/sharing, no PPU profiling. Streaming RMSNorm apply-only stage; true MACs are 0 and modeled operations are reported separately.

Optimized INT8 RTL-aware model: 8x8 shared systolic, physical buffer lifetime/sharing, no PPU profiling. K/V precompute only: full Q is not stored; current-head K/V buffers are used to reduce BRAM footprint. Ping-pong uses max(compute, DRAM preload, BRAM service) as the latency lower bound.

Optimized INT8 RTL-aware model: 8x8 shared systolic, physical buffer lifetime/sharing, no PPU profiling. Includes PH_Q_TILE recomputation before QK^T. Only a small current-query Q cache is live; full Q is not stored. Ping-pong uses max(compute, DRAM preload, BRAM service) as the latency lower bound.

Optimized INT8 RTL-aware model: 8x8 shared systolic, physical buffer lifetime/sharing, no PPU profiling. Score/A share BRAM; row input/output is streamed. Softmax true MACs are 0. The operations column reports modeled LUT/fixed-point row-wise cost with lut_ii=1, norm_ii=1. Optimized LUT Softmax uses row-wise FSM latency: rows*((2+lut_ii+norm_ii)*Tp+overhead) = 1200*((2+1+1)*200+1).

Optimized INT8 RTL-aware model: 8x8 shared systolic, physical buffer lifetime/sharing, no PPU profiling. A from shared Score/A BRAM and current-head V from u_v_xmid_bram; O_attn is written into u_norm_bram. Ping-pong uses max(compute, DRAM preload, BRAM service) as the latency lower bound.

Optimized INT8 RTL-aware model: 8x8 shared systolic, physical buffer lifetime/sharing, no PPU profiling. O_attn is read from u_norm_bram; residual X is read from u_x_bram; X_mid is written into u_v_xmid_bram. Ping-pong uses max(compute, DRAM preload, BRAM service) as the latency lower bound.

Optimized INT8 RTL-aware model: 8x8 shared systolic, physical buffer lifetime/sharing, no PPU profiling. FC1+GELU uses a 1K-word page BRAM and DDR page streaming. Full hidden_gelu is not kept on chip; FC2 hidden DDR reload factor is 48. Ping-pong uses max(compute, DRAM preload, BRAM service) as the latency lower bound.
